title: RnS: watcher writes its own pidfile (liveness confusion hardening)
description: Three separate watcher-liveness investigations happened today (limo's TASK-090 caveat, lema's TASK-095 review stale-pidfile finding, zaro's post-release check) because the pidfile is only written by the start script's shell $! and can drift from reality -- it was found containing literally '9' while the watcher (pid 520835) ran healthy. Harden: limit_watcher.py writes os.getpid() to the pidfile at startup in its long-running mode (not --once/--dry-run), so the file is authoritative for whichever process actually runs, regardless of how it was started (script, tmux, lab autostart, manual).
acceptance_criteria: Watcher main loop writes its own pid to .locks/limit-watcher.pid at startup; --once and --dry-run do not touch it
Existing 15 watcher tests still pass; suite green
